title: Chilita eriacantha
taxonomy: Chilita eriacantha belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This plant is native to Central and South America. It is classified as a species of flowering plant, specifically a shrub or small tree. Chilita eriacantha is also known by its other name, Psychotria viridis, which is a synonym for this species. Further classification can be broken down into more specific categories, such as genus and species.
uses: Chilita eriacantha, also known as the false dragon's blood tree, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves and bark are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In some cultures, Chilita eriacantha is believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it useful for wound healing. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications and potential side effects.

Its bark is a rich source of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. parts: Chilita eriacantha is a plant native to South America, and its medicinal parts are used to treat various health issues. side_effects: Chilita eriacantha is reported to have potential side effects, including gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and stomach upset, due to its toxic compounds.
